exampleSentencesTranslated: You just messaged someone, playful teasing.
You bought me a gift without telling me first, playful teasing.
You went out to eat with someone special last night, playful teasing.
notes_md:
**“Á nha”** is a playful “aha, got you!” when you catch someone doing something. It’s teasing, a little suspicious, but not serious.

It’s a sentence‑ending particle, added after a full statement to show you noticed something sneaky, cute, or secret. Tone goes slightly rising and playful.  

It often carries a light warning vibe, like “I see what you did.” Similar feeling to **“à ha”** (aha) but softer and more teasing.

exampleSentences: Anh vừa thấy em nhắn tin cho ai đó, á nha.
Chị mua quà cho em mà không nói trước, á nha.
Bạn đi ăn với người đặc biệt tối qua, á nha.
